title: [rootless+CNI] `podman run -p` is flaky (because `lo` in the container isn't brought up sometimes)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

`podman run -p` is flaky when running with rootless CNI

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create foo`

2. `podman run -d -p 8080:80 --network foo nginx:alpine`

3. `curl http://localhost:8080/`

**Describe the results you received:**

Sometimes it works ("Welcome to nginx" page), sometimes no response

**Describe the results you expected:**

Should work always

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The issue happens only with `--network <CNI>`.
It does not happen with `--network slirp4netns`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.0-dev
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Git Commit:   25fb0c2b966c193e8c4045e440316850f6f1b889
Built:        Sat Sep 12 17:25:34 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
Seems hanging here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/852943516606f32ccc2406f41bcf3df42d7c622c/vendor/github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit/pkg/port/builtin/parent/tcp/tcp.go#L58

The function above seems hanging here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/852943516606f32ccc2406f41bcf3df42d7c622c/vendor/github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit/pkg/port/builtin/msg/msg.go#L70

This happen when `lo` interface inside the container isn't brought up for some reason.

```console
$ podman run -d --name foo  -p 8080:80 --network foo nginx:alpine
812042b53c4249dc5b3be8f6da594928c516aefd5df2e5f34dadd8c1b9acde6e
$ podman exec foo ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK> mtu 65536 qdisc noop state DOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
3: eth0@if39: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether ae:88:9d:11:8e:b0 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.2.38/24 brd 10.88.2.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::ac88:9dff:fe11:8eb0/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```

So rootlesskit and slirp4netns are probably innocent :) 



@vrothberg @giuseppe @rhatdan PTAL?
